note: Follow-up data for the q.econ.inequality flagship. Freely-fetchable sources are ingested by scripts/ingest-inequality.mjs; the authoritative HCES tables by scripts/ingest-hces-published.mjs (see data/catalog/inequality-manifest.json). The items below need a login, a manual download, or extra processing, so they are listed for later.
fetchedDirectly: World Bank API: consumption Gini, decile/quintile shares, $2.15 poverty
OWID grapher (World Inequality Database): top 1% / top 10% income share (1922-2022), top 1% / top 10% wealth share, WID-vs-World-Bank top-1% comparison
OWID grapher (World Bank): consumption distribution bottom-50 / middle-40 / top-10

HCES 2023-24 press note (PDF snapshot in data/snapshots/mospi-hces/): MPCE levels (with/without imputation), 12-class fractile MPCE distribution, 18-state MPCE rural+urban, Gini 0.237/0.284 — all authoritative published tables; consumption shares computed from official fractile means
World Inequality Lab WP 2024/09: headline 2022-23 income & wealth distribution (transcribed — VERIFY against paper tables)

gaps.source: MoSPI HCES 2023-24 unit-level microdata — own MPCE build-up
gaps.status: DONE for caste & religion. scripts/derive-hces-granular.py builds MPCE with MMRP reference-period scaling (7-day x30/7 for sec 6-7; 365-day x30/365 for education/medical-hosp/clothing/durables; 30-day else), reverse-engineered and confirmed via Table 3.5 (food total reproduces Rs 1,939/2,776 exactly). Validated vs 18 published state means at 3.6% MAPE, correct rank order; calibrated to published national means. Population shares match census (OBC 46%, SC 19%, ST 10%; Hindu 82%, Muslim 13%). Wired as econ.inequality.hces_mpce_by_caste / _by_religion (tableBars charts 11-12). Derived JSON: data/snapshots/mospi-hces/hces_granular_derived.json.
gaps.remaining: District-level (689 districts computed, MPCE range Rs 1,189-13,701) not charted — needs a state+district code->name map. Finer percentiles also possible. ~11% of MPCE sits in ambiguous sections (pan/tobacco/misc/rent/taxes) so absolute levels carry +/- a few %; gaps & Ginis are robust.

gaps.source: PLFS 2023-24 + quarterly 2025 unit-level data (wages, education, social group)
gaps.status: DONE. scripts/derive-plfs-wages.py + ingest-plfs-wages.mjs. Earnings field mapped from Stata variable labels (b6q9=regular wage/salaried monthly; b6q10=self-employed). VALIDATED exactly: regular wage Rs 21,048 vs published ~21,000; self-emp 13,363 vs ~13,300; gender ratio 0.74. Joined to hhv1 caste(b3q4)/religion(b3q3) with 0 unmatched. Wired as plfs_wage_by_caste / plfs_gender_ratio_by_caste / plfs_wage_by_religion (charts 13-15).

gaps.source: All-India Debt & Investment Survey (AIDIS, NSSO 77th round, 2019)
gaps.whyItMatters: India's only official ASSET survey — the domestic counterpart to the WID wealth estimates, and the honest 'official' wealth-inequality number to set beside the modelled 40.1% top-1% figure.

gaps.source: World Inequality Database — bottom-50% income share time series
gaps.access: WID.world bulk download or wid R/Stata package (the public REST API returned 403 in this run; OWID has no India-facing grapher slug for it)
gaps.whyItMatters: Completes the income-distribution picture (we have top 1% and top 10%; the bottom-50 line is the missing third).

gaps.source: UBS Global Wealth Report 2024/2025 (wealth-per-adult, country inequality ranking)
gaps.access: Manual PDF download from ubs.com; not an open API; model-based — cite with care
gaps.whyItMatters: International benchmarking ('India as unequal as the US on wealth') and the millionaires-surge angle.

gaps.source: Forbes / Hurun India rich lists (billionaire count & combined wealth)
gaps.whyItMatters: The top-tail anchor (271 billionaires, +94 in 2023) that makes wealth concentration concrete.

gaps.source: NFHS-5 wealth index by social group; PLFS wage gaps by sex/caste; IHDS
gaps.access: NFHS-5 already partly in repo (NFHS-6 hub); PLFS microdata via NADA; IHDS via ICPSR/free login
gaps.whyItMatters: The 'inequalities the Gini hides' act — caste, religion, gender, region — which is Indica's differentiation from generic Gini coverage.
gaps.status: PARTLY DONE: PLFS wage gaps by caste+gender+religion shipped (see plfs-microdata). Remaining: NFHS wealth-index by social group, IHDS.

gaps.source: Per-capita Net State Domestic Product (MOSPI eSankhyiki)
gaps.access: Open MOSPI API (api.mospi.gov.in) — already used elsewhere in repo
gaps.whyItMatters: Regional inequality — Bihar vs Karnataka per-capita gaps rival country-level differences; a state map/bar would be a strong addition.

verificationTODOs: HCES rural/urban Gini values (0.283/0.363 in 2011-12; 0.266/0.314 in 2022-23; 0.237/0.284 in 2023-24) are transcribed from the PIB press factsheet — confirm against the official HCES 2023-24 factsheet PDF before publish.

WIL headline shares (income: 15 / 27.3 / 57.7 / top1 22.6; wealth: 6.4 / 28.6 / 65 / top1 40.1) are from secondary summaries of WP 2024/09 — confirm against the paper's own tables; the middle-40 wealth share is a residual.
